quality.rationale: 结合经典引力与旧量子化条件的量纲估算，步骤完整但属非相对论思想实验

origin.question: Problem 12.19. Estimate the Bohr radius of a "gravitational atom", e.g. two neutrons bound by their gravitational attraction in their lowest energy state.
origin.answer: r = 2 \hbar^ {2} / G m _ {N} ^ {3} = 6 \times 1 0 ^ {2 4} c m \approx 6 \times 1 0 ^ {6} l i g h t y e a r s!
origin.solution: Solution 12.19. Force balance gives us$$\mathrm {G m}_{\mathbf {N}}^{2} / \mathrm {r}^{2} = \mathrm {m}_{\mathbf {N}} \omega^{2} \left(\frac {1}{2} \mathrm {r}\right).$$According to Bohr quantization the separation radius must satisfy$$2 m \omega \left(\frac {1}{2} r\right)^{2} = n \pi .$$From these two equations, the separation is$$r = 2 n^{2} \hbar^{2} / G m_{N}^{3}.$$The lowest energy level is the $n = 1$ level. In this level the separation is$$r = 2 \hbar^{2} / G m_{N}^{3} = 6 \times 1 0^{2 4} c m \approx 6 \times 1 0^{6} l i g h t y e a r s!$$
